title: Session display Phase 3 — Detach (detachable windows)
body:
## Requirement

**Session display Phase 3 — Detach: detachable session windows.** Move a session into its own OS window (draggable to a 2nd monitor), session keeps running; reattach back into the main window.

- **Legacy stays the default** (non-breaking); detach is an **action**, not a third exclusive mode. Controlled via setting `global.sessionDisplayMode`.
- Builds on **Phase 1 tabs (#6)** + **Phase 2 settings window (#7)** (both done) — uses the multi-window pattern proven there.
- Central change: route `terminal-data` per session to the owning window (sidebar/attention events stay in the main window).

Detailed design and implementation plan → comment.

title: Terminal: working-status line renders stacked/duplicated (rare) — distinct from #85 garble
body:
## Requirement

The Claude working-status line occasionally renders **stacked/duplicated** — several
successive status frames sit on top of each other (e.g. three `Hashing…` lines with
*different* content: `22 24`, `16s ↓401`, `11s ↓138 · thought for 5s`) instead of the
single line overwriting in place. A resize heals it. **Occurs rarely.** Seen on build
`main @ 7e473e7` (which already carries the DPR-refit fix), WebGL off (DOM renderer).

This is **distinct from #85** (that was the horizontal char-drift *garble* = xterm.js#6015,
addressed by the DPR-refit). This is **vertical duplication** — old status rows not cleared
when the TUI rewrites the status line — a different mechanism.

**Candidate:** xterm.js#5801 (OPEN) — "ED2 (`\x1b[2J`) inside a DEC 2026 synchronized update
block resets viewportY, causing scroll yank during streaming redraws." Matches stacked status
frames during sync redraws. To be confirmed.

**Expected:** a single, in-place-updating status line; no stacking.

## Diagnosis (in progress)

Temporary probe added on a debug branch (never merged): **F5** = pure `terminal.refresh(0, rows-1)`
(render repaint), **F8** = `safeFit` (geometry re-fit) on the focused session. Press each on the
stacked ghost:
- F5 clears it → dirty-row render-ghost → a refresh-heal fixes it.
- F5 fails, F8/resize clears it → geometry/re-fit class.
- Neither → buffer/sync-viewport (xterm.js#5801) → upstream / deeper.

Keep open until the mechanism is identified.

title: WebGL context loss leaves a stale fit (no re-fit, no repaint)
body:
`WebglAddon.onContextLoss` (`public/terminal-manager.js`) disposes the addon and marks the DOM renderer as suggested, but never re-fits the terminal.

xterm silently continues on its DOM renderer, whose cell metrics differ from WebGL's (xterm.js#6015). Every other renderer switch compensates: `loadTerminalWebgl()` calls `safeFit()` after loading, and `showSession()` re-fits on the next frame when it restores a context suspended by the grid's off-screen handling. The loss path does neither.

Result: a visible terminal that loses its GL context keeps a fit computed for WebGL cell metrics while rendering with DOM metrics — the bottom row can clip, the same failure mode #81 traced to renderer swaps under fractional display scaling. It self-heals only on the next tab switch, when `showSession()` reloads WebGL and re-fits.

Raising the GL-context budget to 32 narrows the window but does not close it: driver-side losses still occur.

### Acceptance
- `onContextLoss` re-fits the terminal and forces a full repaint.
- The re-fit is deferred by one frame — cell metrics are reported only after the renderer swap has painted, matching the existing restore path.
- No re-fit is attempted on a terminal destroyed between the loss and the frame.

Split out of #81 (found while re-reviewing its WebGL invariants).

title: Onboarding: first-time-launch welcome screen
body:
Show a welcome screen on the very first launch to orient a new user, then never again unless reset.

## Requirement

- On first launch (fresh install — no global settings yet), show a welcome overlay: brief intro to the app + primary actions.
- **Actions**: "Import settings" (ties into settings import — migrate from another machine) and "Open settings" (first-time configuration), plus a Dismiss/close.
- Dismissing persists a flag so it never shows again (store `welcomeDismissed` in the global settings blob via `merge-setting`, so it rides existing persistence + export/import).
- Style matches existing dialogs (`new-session-overlay` pattern). UI text English.

Depends on the settings export/import feature for the "Import settings" action.

Plan/design in comments.

title: Claude: resuming a session that a live process owns dies with a raw CLI error
body:
## Problem

Switchboard offers **Resume** on every Claude session, including one that a **live process currently owns** — a background agent (`claude --bg`), or an interactive session running in a terminal outside Switchboard. Claude refuses to open such a session twice, so the spawn dies immediately:

```
Session <id> is currently running as a background agent (bg). Use `claude agents` to find and
attach to it, or add --fork-session to branch off a copy.
── session exited (code 1) ──
```

The user clicked an action the app was never able to perform. The spawn path already refuses a launch it knows will fail — a disabled backend, a missing binary, a fork of an id the backend does not know yet (main.js, the guards before `buildLaunch`) — this case is simply not among them.

Two rows in the sidebar can also carry the **same name** (Claude writes the same auto `ai-title` into both transcripts when a background agent and an interactive session work on the same topic), and nothing distinguishes them. It reads as a duplicate entry; it is two sessions, one of which is live and unresumable.

## Requirement

- Do not spawn a resume that cannot succeed. Refuse it before the PTY exists, with a sentence that says why and what to do instead — the way the other pre-spawn guards do.
- Offer the alternative Claude itself names: **fork a copy** (`--resume <id> --fork-session`), which Switchboard already supports.
- Make a session that is currently running elsewhere recognisable in the sidebar, so two identically named rows can be told apart.
- Ownership must be asked of the **backend**, not hardcoded: a backend that cannot answer the question keeps today's behaviour.

## Notes

- Claude answers it directly: `claude agents --json` prints the live sessions (documented in its `--help` as "for scripting; does not require a TTY") — one entry per session with `sessionId`, `kind` (`background` | `interactive`), `pid`, `status`, `name`, `cwd`.
- It costs **~1.5 s** per call, so it cannot go on the scan path uncached.
- `~/.claude/jobs/<short-id>/state.json` records the same session (`template: "bg"`), but its `state` reads `done` while the daemon still holds the session — the job state is NOT a liveness signal. The agent list is.

title: Add Codex support to scheduled tasks (Claude + Codex)
body:
Scheduled tasks work only for Claude today — the scheduler is Claude-hardcoded (`schedule-runner.js` / `schedule-ipc.js`: `claude … -p`, schedule defs in `<project>/.claude/commands/schedule-<slug>.md`, pre-seed a Claude JSONL to `--resume` into).

Of the other backends, **only Codex makes sense** for unattended scheduling: `codex exec` is a genuine agentic headless runner (tools/edits, multi-step), auto-persists a rollout the scanner already indexes, gives clean auto-approve (`-a never`), and authenticates from an env key without a human. **agy, pi and hermes are OUT of scope** (per the assessment comment) — their unattended-auth or headless semantics make a cron run fragile or unverified.

**Goal:** after this, scheduled tasks support **Claude and Codex — and only those.**

**Requirement:** add Codex as a schedulable backend, driven by the backend descriptor (no hardcoded backend argv), generalizing the scheduler just enough for these two.

**Acceptance:**
- Codex scheduled tasks work end-to-end: create → cron run (`codex exec` headless, `-a never`) → the run shows as a Codex session (its rollout is auto-indexed by the scanner — no pre-seed/resume needed).
- The scheduler is no longer Claude-only: a descriptor hook (e.g. `buildScheduleLaunch`) supplies the headless invocation + auto-approve flags per backend; no backend id hardcoded outside its own folder (CLAUDE.md rule).
- Schedule definitions live in a **backend-neutral store** (backendId + prompt + cron + per-backend option overrides), not only Claude's `.claude/commands/`. Slug-grouping of repeated runs (Claude-only field today) gets a Switchboard-side map.
- Claude scheduling keeps working unchanged.
- agy, pi, hermes are **not** added — explicitly out of scope (documented in the assessment).

title: Variable quick-pick: keyboard-driven (type-to-filter, arrow nav, Enter inserts + trailing space)
body:
The variable quick-pick popover already opens from the focused terminal via the existing `insertVariable` hotkey (**Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+V**, rebindable) and from the terminal-header key button. But it is **mouse-only**: inside the popover only Escape does anything — there is no filtering and no keyboard selection. You have to click a row's Insert button.

**Requirement:** make the quick-pick keyboard-driven, command-palette style, reusing the existing hotkey (no new binding):
- A **filter input**, focused on open; typing filters the list live (case-insensitive substring over the variable name).
- **Arrow ↑ / ↓** moves a highlighted selection through the filtered list (wrapping optional).
- **Enter inserts** the highlighted variable's resolved value **followed by a single trailing space, no newline** (distinct from Send, which adds a newline).
- **Escape** closes (unchanged). Send / Copy stay available (mouse, or a modifier on Enter).

**Acceptance:**
- Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+V opens the popover with the filter focused.
- Typing filters; ↑/↓ moves the highlight; Enter inserts `<value> ` (value + one trailing space, no newline) into the terminal; Escape closes.
- Secret variables resolve through the existing insert path (no plaintext leak change).
- No new hotkey is added — the existing `insertVariable` binding is reused.

title: demo:start isolates where Switchboard looks, not where the CLI writes — a live session lands in the real store
body:
`npm run demo:start` isolates where **Switchboard looks** for sessions (`SWITCHBOARD_STORE_CLAUDE` and its
four siblings), but not where **the CLI writes** them. Claude resolves its own store from
`CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` (default `~/.claude`), which the demo does not set — so a session actually launched
from the demo instance writes into the user's real `~/.claude/projects`, and the demo app never sees it.

For everything the demo was built for — seeded transcripts, the sidebar, search, stats — this is
invisible. It bites the moment a test needs a **live CLI session**: verifying #223's `/clear` re-key, the
attention hook, live busy/idle, adoption. Those tests currently have to run against `npm start` and the
real store, which is exactly what the demo exists to avoid, and they leave test projects behind in the
user's own history.

The blocker is authentication, not the variable: point `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` at the demo directory and the
CLI is logged out, because the credentials live in that directory (`.credentials.json`, or the OS keychain
under a name derived from the config dir).

## Requirement

- A demo run can launch a REAL backend session whose transcript lands inside the demo directory, not in
  the user's store — for the backends whose home is env-overridable.
- The authentication problem is solved or explicitly documented: either the demo reuses the real
  credentials in a way the user opted into, or `docs/demo-env.md` states plainly that a live session in
  the demo needs a one-time login, and how to do it.
- Whatever is chosen holds for the other CLIs too (`CODEX_HOME`, `HERMES_HOME`,
  `PI_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_DIR`), or the doc says which ones it covers.

## Acceptance

- Starting a session in the demo instance writes its transcript under the demo directory, and the demo
  sidebar shows it.
- The real `~/.claude/projects` is untouched by a demo run — checked, not assumed.
- `docs/demo-env.md` describes the login step (or its absence) rather than leaving the next person to
  discover it the way this was discovered.

title: Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)
body:
## Requirement

**Remote repo over SSH (treat like local)** _(Source: supacode)_ — open a repo/project on a remote host via SSH and manage it in Switchboard like a local one: session scan, terminal (PTY over SSH), file/diff access. **Large** — needs its own detailed plan (transport, auth via Bitwarden SSH agent, remote `~/.claude/projects` scan, PTY bridging, watcher replacement). Clarify scope/architecture before implementation.

title: Detect subagent spawn via .meta.json (not only .jsonl)
body:
## Requirement

**Detect subagent spawn via `.meta.json` (not only `.jsonl`)** — when spawning a task subagent, Claude Code immediately writes `<parent>/subagents/agent-<id>.meta.json` (`agentType`, `description`, `toolUseId`, `spawnDepth`), but the `.jsonl` transcript only once the agent produces output. A spawned agent that did not (yet) run to producing output (queued, parent turn ends/is interrupted) has **only** `meta.json` → `detectSubagentTransitions`/`enumerateSessionFiles` (which only scan `subagents/*.jsonl`) never see it (observed 2026-07-02: session `876c2b28`, "Feldtexte W6" agents — meta identical to runnable W5 agents, but without jsonl; parent last at 22:26, spawns at 22:28). **No special background-agents format, no data loss — pure visibility.** Fix: also scan `subagents/*.meta.json` → placeholder subagent row (name from `description`, status "spawned/empty/aborted") as soon as the meta is there; upgrade to a full row when the `.jsonl` arrives. Files: `read-session-file.js` (`enumerateSessionFiles`), `session-transitions.js`, `session-cache.js`, subagent rendering in `public/sidebar.js`.

title: Make the terminal LRU cap configurable
body:
The renderer keeps at most 12 live xterm instances (`TERMINAL_LRU_CAP` in `public/terminal-manager.js`); the least-recently-shown closed session beyond the cap is destroyed (soft cap: running and active sessions are never evicted).

Since WebGL is the default renderer, the cap also serves as the GL-context budget (Chromium allows ~16 per process; a lost context falls back to the DOM renderer).

Expose the cap as a setting for users with many parallel sessions and enough RAM. Constraints: keep 12 as the default; values above ~14 should hint that terminals beyond the GL budget silently fall back to the DOM renderer.

Deferred from #81 (was rejected there as "defer until the cap is hit").

title: Evaluate: multi-sidebar panes
body: Evaluate porting aaaron `feat/multi-sidebar-panes` — multiple sidebar panes. Source: aaaronmiller/switchboard @ feat/multi-sidebar-panes.

title: Evaluate: local Whisper voice input
body: Evaluate porting ivandobsky `feat/local-whisper-voice` — local Whisper voice control/input. Source: ivandobskygithub/switchboard @ feat/local-whisper-voice.

title: Evaluate: brianstanley cursor-ui-refresh
body: Evaluate brianstanley `cursor-ui-refresh` — UI refresh. Source: brianstanley/switchboard @ cursor-ui-refresh. We already ported brianstanley's Variables + preview panel; check what's new/portable.

title: Optional second tray icon showing Claude usage (live percentage)
body:
Add an optional **second system-tray icon** that shows current Claude usage, enabled via Settings (off by default).

## Requirement
- A live-rendered tray icon showing the current usage percentage, styled like a usage monitor: a **colored progress ring** or a **badge** with the number baked into the icon, color-coded by the existing warn/critical thresholds (green → amber → red).
- **Tooltip** with the details we already show in the status bar (5h / 7d, extra usage, reset time).
- Small **context menu**: show/hide the window, open Stats, refresh, quit.
- **Cross-platform**: macOS, Windows, Linux.
- **Reuse the usage data the app already has** — the same values the status bar renders. **No separate polling loop / no extra network fetch** for the tray; it updates whenever the existing status-bar usage updates.
- Enabled + styled via a **Settings** toggle (icon style ring/badge). When off, the second tray is not created; toggling off destroys it.

Reference behaviour: a small pystray + Pillow usage-monitor tray that draws a ring/badge with the percentage and threshold colors.

title: Terminal: evaluate VSCode-style shell integration (OSC 633) for plain terminals
body:
Idea from the VSCode draw-path comparison (#87/#114 context). VSCode's shell integration injects a shell profile hook that emits OSC 633 sequences (command start / command end / exit code / cwd), enabling command tracking, "run recent command", per-command scrollback navigation, and precise prompt detection.

**Requirement:** evaluate whether a slim variant is worth it for our **plain terminals** (not Claude sessions — the CLI is a fullscreen TUI where command tracking is meaningless): e.g. exit-code badges, jump-to-previous-command in scrollback, or more reliable "shell is idle" detection. Likely low value for this app's core use case; document a decision either way. Large feature — only pursue with a concrete user-facing benefit.

title: i18n: renderer localisation support layer (language-neutral, EN master)
body:
Add a language-neutral localisation **support layer** to the renderer, so any user-facing UI string can be translated. This issue is about the **infrastructure**, not the translations themselves — English stays the master/source of truth; concrete languages are added on top afterwards.

## Requirement (the support layer)

- A global `t('area.key', { placeholders })` available to every renderer script — no build step, no module system (matches our vanilla `<script>` setup).
- **English is the master.** Missing keys fall back to English, then to the key itself — the UI is never blank.
- Static HTML localised via `data-i18n` / `data-i18n-title` / `data-i18n-placeholder` / `data-i18n-aria` attributes, filled on load.
- Active language is a global setting (Settings UI). Changing it persists and re-renders every view in the new language.
- Extract the existing English UI strings (sidebar tabs/chrome, session filters, settings labels/dialogs/tooltips) into the dictionary, keyed. No behaviour change while only EN exists.
- Language-neutral by design: adding a language = adding one dictionary, no code changes.

## Rollout (separate follow-up issues, not this one)

Language order once the layer lands: **EN (master) → DE → FR**. Each added language is its own translation task on top of this support layer.

## Work organisation

Large, cross-cutting change (touches nearly every renderer file that emits text) → **own feature branch**.

Reference implementation for the layer: kreaddis `public/i18n.js` (~488 lines) — adapt the mechanism, ignore its specific fr strings. Upstream commit `6e4d127` (fr apostrophe fix) is only relevant once translations exist.

Plan/design + string-extraction strategy go in comments.

title: Session provenance: show where a session came from (clear heuristic + explicit parent links)
body:
Switchboard shows sessions as a flat list, but many sessions are not fresh starts — they were spun out of an earlier session and lose that link in the UI. Surface a session's **provenance**: where it came from, so a session that continues another's work reads as such instead of as an unrelated new thread.

The relationship exists in the transcripts, but it is recorded differently per backend, and one case is not recorded at all:

- **Claude `/clear`** starts a brand-new session with a new id and writes **no** back-link — the only trace is the `/clear` command at the head of the child file. Provenance here can only be **heuristic**.
- **Claude / Codex compaction** stays in the *same* session and is already linked (`logicalParentUuid` + `isCompactSummary` for Claude; an inline `compacted` row with `replacement_history` for Codex) — no heuristic needed.
- **Pi** writes an explicit `parentSession` (full path to the parent transcript) in its session header.
- **Hermes** carries an explicit `parent_session_id` column.

Requirement:

1. Where a backend records lineage explicitly (Pi `parentSession`, Hermes `parent_session_id`), show it as a **hard** link ("continued from …").
2. For **Claude `/clear`**, where no link exists, derive a **soft** heuristic guess and label it as a guess, never as fact. Detect the boundary from `/clear` at the head of the child session; pick the most likely parent from same `cwd` + same `gitBranch` + the nearest preceding session that ended just before the child began. On ambiguity (parallel sessions in the same cwd), show nothing rather than guess wrong.
3. Optionally surface compaction as "continued after compaction" from the links that already exist.

Backend-neutral: the lineage source is a per-backend detail (a descriptor hook), not a hardcoded `if (backendId === …)` in the core. A backend with no lineage concept simply contributes nothing.

title: agy backend: add a usage capability (status bar + Usage & Notifications), like Claude/Codex
body:
The `agy` backend ships without a `usage` capability (#191), so it has no usage figure in the status bar and no entry under **Settings → Usage & Notifications** — unlike Claude and Codex. The descriptor documents this as a deferred follow-up (`backends/agy/index.js:165-167`): agy's quota is per-model with no clean local file (unlike Codex's `token_count`), so a real usage bar needs a network fetch. It currently ships `ready` without one, like Hermes and Pi.

**Requirement:** agy exposes a `usage` capability matching the #191 contract — a `backends/agy/usage.js` with a `fetch()`, declared as `usage: { live, fetch }` in the descriptor — so its quota shows in the status bar and under Usage & Notifications the same way Claude and Codex do. Everything else derives from the descriptor (the poll loop in `main.js` already enumerates every `enabled` backend whose descriptor has `usage.fetch`).

**Acceptance:**
- With agy enabled, the status bar shows its usage and Settings → Usage & Notifications lists agy, consistent with Claude/Codex.
- `test/backend-parity.test.js` and the usage tests stay green.

title: perf: run the live-session identity match (matchLiveSession) in the index worker
body:
## Problem

#209 measured the on-main cost of `matchLiveSession` (the birth-time identity match that pairs a freshly
spawned Axis-B session with the transcript the CLI just named) and cut it with a filename-derived birth
pre-filter — 82% off a 5000-transcript store. What is left is the part a filter cannot remove: the
**`discoverSessions()` readdir walk itself**, plus the stat of every transcript born inside the skew margin.

Measured after the #209 fix, per watcher flush while a session is still unpaired, on the main thread:

| transcripts | matchLiveSession |
|---|---|
| 500 | ~17 ms |
| 2000 | ~27 ms |
| 5000 | ~44 ms |

Bounded (it stops the moment the session pairs, normally after 1–2 flushes) and an order below the stall
#199 removed — but it is the last store walk the main thread still does, and it grows with the store.

## Requirement

Run the identity match off the main thread, through the persistent index worker: main posts the match
request (backendId + cwd + sinceMs + the claimed set), the worker runs `matchLiveSession` (it is
Electron-free and the worker already resolves backends by id), main claims the reply.

Known costs to design for, none of them blockers, all of them the reason this is filed rather than done:

- **`claimLiveRecord` becomes async**, and `updateBackendLiveStates` is today a synchronous loop that
  re-keys `activeSessions` mid-iteration. The re-key would move to a later tick — the snapshot-to-avoid-
  mutation logic has to be rethought.
- **New race surface**, the same shape #208 needed guards for: a session that exits while its match is in
  flight; two flushes posting two match requests for one session (needs per-session coalescing); a stale
  reply arriving after another claimed the record.
- **Latency**: the id binding and the first busy/idle push wait a worker round-trip, so a brand-new
  non-Claude session shows its temp id a beat longer. Claude's temp→real transition already tolerates this.
- **The walk is relocated, not removed** — the worker then pays it, and a match competing with the reconcile
  and file lanes needs a sensible place in the priority queue.

## Acceptance

- No store walk or transcript read runs on the main thread in the backend-store watcher flush.
- A freshly launched Codex/Pi/agy session still binds to its real session id and shows live busy/idle,
  without a restart; a resumed session still claims its record via `liveRefFor`.
- `npm test` green.

Follow-up to #209 (design record + the measured numbers: `docs/specs/11-performance.md`).

title: Session lineage — remaining work (provenance + /clear re-key)
body:
Central place to continue the **session lineage / provenance** feature (#193 + #223). What shipped, and what is left.

## Shipped (on `main`)

Lineage is **backend-neutral**: one sink (`src/index/index-writes.js applyIndexResults`) stamps `lineageParentId`/`lineageKind` by calling `backends.get(backendId).resolveLineage(row)` — the core never reads a backend's format or branches on an id. Each descriptor declares the hook (Claude → fork, Hermes → parent, Codex/Pi/agy → `null` honest gaps). Rendering (Model A, `src/renderer/shell/sidebar-lineage.js`): the live/newest session is the head, idle ancestors fold under a `▶ N earlier` caret (the subagent-nesting affordance), each ancestor a **full session row** with all its actions; a live ancestor stays its own row. Spec 13 + `docs/backend-formats.md` document it. Verified by an adversarial verifier (twice) + live in the app.

Commits: `fab1efc`, `5066e2b` (#223 resolver), `52fed15`, `9c1ff59` (#193 data + first render), `85b8d4d`, `83e4450` (neutral refactor + profile fix), `4dc7cae`, `98d7aff`, `b974db6`, `8341c86` (rendering: caret → drop caption → full rows → docs). CLAUDE.md now records the neutral-descriptor rule for any cross-backend feature.

## Open — the actual continuation work

- [ ] **The multi-session `/clear` re-key (#223 headline) is NOT solved.** With ≥2 live sessions in one folder the source keeps its row/tab until it exits. No folder-local signal (mtime, cwd, gitBranch) can tell the true parent from a just-idle bystander — the first cut mtime-heuristic mis-keyed and was reverted to a conservative single-session-only rule. Needs a signal that ties a `/clear` to a specific PTY (e.g. a per-session `SessionStart` hook echo that names the parent — the hook fires but does not carry the parent today). This is the biggest piece.
- [ ] **Wire lineage for the backends that currently return `null`** — only when the signal is verified against the real format (see the CLAUDE.md rule): Codex (`/clear` starts a new rollout with no back-ref; `compacted` is a state, not a parent), Pi (`--fork` exists but the header records no parent), agy (`parent_references` is an unschema'd protobuf blob — reverse-engineer against a real forked trajectory). Each is a descriptor + reader edit, no core change.
- [ ] **Hard/soft visual marker** — the hard (fork/parent) vs soft (`/clear` guess) distinction lives only in the data (`lineageKind`) now; there is no visual cue. Cheap follow-up if wanted: a dimmed/italic ancestor row (or a subtle badge) for `lineageKind === 'clear'`. Deliberately deferred as "not necessary".
- [ ] **Cosmetic**: an expanded thread collapses on the next sidebar re-render (morphdom re-applies `display:none`); a very long `/clear` chain is not capped in the expander; a live ancestor also appears inside a descendant's expander (consistent with Model A's shared-ancestor stance).
- [ ] **Same-session compaction** (Claude `logicalParentUuid`, Codex `compacted`) is out of scope — not a cross-session parent, so not a lineage row. Noted so it is not re-litigated.

Related: #223 (the re-key), #193 (the display).
